_fields: id, title, source, source_url, description, target_audience, pain_point, market_size_estimate, existing_competitors, competitive_gap, monetization_angle, confidence_score, timestamp, raw_signals
_rules: APPEND ONLY — never overwrite or truncate this file

title: Minimalist Habit Streak Tracker
description: A minimalist habit tracker focused on streak mechanics and motivation without overwhelming features or anxiety-inducing pressure. Addresses key gaps in current market where users abandon complex apps after initial enthusiasm.
target_audience: Adults 18-45 seeking simple habit formation tools, particularly those frustrated with over-complicated existing apps, productivity-focused individuals who prefer minimalist design
pain_point: Existing habit trackers are too complex (15+ features), create anxiety around streak breaks, have data persistence issues, and cap free users at 3-7 habits causing abandonment

market_size_estimate.methodology: Based on market research showing 52% user drop-off rate in 30 days, with 44% citing complexity/overwhelm as primary reason. Minimalist segment represents underserved 15-20% of total addressable market.

competitive_gap: No major competitor focuses specifically on anxiety-free streak mechanics. Current leaders either over-gamify (Habitica), over-complicate (Habitify), or limit free features (Streaks). Users specifically request 'unlimited habits', 'less demotivating streaks', and 'data persistence reliability'
raw_signals: Reddit: 'Too many habits at once — Starting with drink water, exercise, meditate, read, journal, call mom, stretch, learn Spanish is setting yourself up to fail'
User review: 'Streak anxiety kills motivation — Miss one day and your 47-day streak is gone'
Market data: '52% drop-off rate in 30 days, 44% lose motivation, 39% churn from lack of personalization'
User feedback: 'Free versions are too limited — Apps advertise free habit tracking but cap you at 3-7 habits'
Reddit: 'Loop is my absolute favourite. It doesn't stuff any features behind a pay wall, has decent widgets and has backups'

title: YieldSentinel — Real-Time Credit Ratings for Crypto Yield Products
description: A Bloomberg Terminal-style monitoring service that continuously rates the health of every crypto yield product via proprietary Sentinel Score (0-100), monitoring collateral ratios, reserve audits, on-chain flows, and alerts users before Celsius-type collapses occur. Trained on historical collapse data (Celsius, Voyager, BlockFi, Terra/Luna, FTX).
target_audience: Crypto funds, family offices, RIA firms, retail yield farmers, institutional crypto investors
pain_point: Existing yield products lack independent risk ratings; Celsius/Voyager/BlockFi collapses caught users off-guard with no predictive warnings. Exchanges won't rate competitors. No Moody's equivalent for DeFi.

market_size_estimate.methodology: Based on $100B+ yield farming TVL, institutional crypto adoption, and comparable financial intelligence services (Bloomberg Terminal $24K/year). Conservative retail projection: 300-4000 users across tiers at $14.99-$499/mo. Conservative Year 1: $55K, Moderate: $380K, Aggressive: $1.15M.

competitive_gap: No independent credit rating agency for DeFi. Competitors provide data dashboards but not predictive risk models. Historical collapses were not predicted by any existing tool. Moat: proprietary algorithm + 5+ years of collapse data training.
raw_signals: User pain: 'Celsius collapse caught users off-guard despite public financials'
Market: '$5B+ institutional capital exposed to unrated yield products'
Expert feedback: 'Moody's for DeFi — proprietary risk scoring — institutional play at $499/mo'
Defensibility: 'Proprietary risk model + 5 years collapse data + no exchange will rate competitors'
Revenue model: 'Freemium, Analyst ($14.99/mo), Professional ($49.99/mo), Institutional ($499/mo), projected $55K-$1.15M annual revenue Year 1

title: LegacyVault — Cross-Platform Crypto Estate Orchestration
description: The only app that monitors wallet activity across every exchange, IRA custodian, and self-custody wallet — and automatically executes your crypto succession plan when you can't. Integrates 10+ exchanges, on-chain monitoring, dormancy detection, multi-signature beneficiary verification, and dead-man switch.
target_audience: High-net-worth crypto holders >$10K seeking estate planning, crypto-native families, individuals concerned with succession planning, beneficiaries
pain_point: Crypto held across multiple platforms (Coinbase, Kraken, iTrustCapital, self-custody) with no unified succession plan. If holder dies or becomes incapacitated, beneficiaries cannot access assets across all platforms. No single platform manages cross-platform succession.

market_size_estimate.tam: $50B+ (every crypto holder with >$10K across all platforms)
market_size_estimate.methodology: Estimated 5-10M high-net-worth crypto holders globally, 20-30% actively considering succession planning. Average estate value $100K+. Comparable: legal estate planning services ($2K-10K per client). App pricing $9.99-29.99/mo scales to multi-billion addressable market.

competitive_gap: No single app monitors across all exchanges + self-custody + beneficiary verification. Moat: read-only API integration to 10+ platforms simultaneously, dormancy detection algorithms, multi-signature beneficiary verification. Each new exchange integration increases switching cost.
raw_signals: Market: 'Every crypto holder thinks about this but has no solution'
Defensibility: 'No single platform has incentive to monitor competitor platforms — LegacyVault sits above all'
TAM: 'Millions of crypto holders, especially those >$100K estates'
Revenue: 'Free, Guardian ($9.99/mo), Estate ($29.99/mo), Family Office ($99.99/mo). Year 1: Conservative $65K, Moderate $320K, Aggressive $900K'
Viral potential: 'Emotional hook — protect your family — fastest path to viral distribution'

title: TreasuryPilot — Multi-Entity Crypto Tax & Compliance Dashboard
description: The only app that gives family offices, trusts, and multi-LLC structures a single pane of glass for crypto holdings across entities with real-time tax-lot tracking, automated quarterly estimated tax calculations, and consolidated reporting. Handles entity-relationship modeling, per-entity tax treatment, wash sale detection across related entities.
target_audience: Multi-entity crypto holders (trusts, family offices, LLCs), crypto-focused CPAs, estate attorneys, accounting firms managing high-net-worth clients
pain_point: CoinTracker and Koinly handle individual tax reporting but choke on multi-entity structures (trust → LLC → individual IRA with different cost basis methods, different tax treatments). No tool handles entity-relationship model or wash sale detection across related entities. CPAs manually reconcile across 4+ platforms.

market_size_estimate.methodology: Estimated 10K-50K multi-entity crypto holders globally with combined $100K-$10M+ holdings. Average ARPU $55-75/mo significantly higher than retail (multi-entity complexity justifies premium pricing). Comparable: CoinTracker Pro $20/mo + CPA fees $2K-$10K/year.

competitive_gap: No tool models entity-relationship hierarchies or enforces per-entity tax-lot tracking with correct cost basis methods (FIFO/LIFO/HIFO per entity). Moat: deep domain knowledge of entity taxation (trust, LLC, S-Corp, IRA), automated wash sale detection across related entities.
raw_signals: CPA pain: 'Need consolidated reporting for multi-entity clients'
Market: 'Higher ARPU than retail — $55-75/mo justified by complexity'
TAM: 'Tens of thousands multi-entity holders, high lifetime value'
Revenue: 'Free, Professional ($29.99/mo), Family Office ($79.99/mo), Enterprise ($199.99/mo). Year 1: Conservative $80K, Moderate $375K, Aggressive $1.08M'
Urgency: 'Tax season creates natural urgency — CPAs will pay immediately before deadlines'

title: LegacyVault — Cross-Platform Crypto Estate Orchestration
description: The only app that monitors wallet activity across every exchange, IRA custodian, and self-custody wallet — and automatically executes crypto succession plans when the user cannot. Sits above all custodians as a cross-platform orchestration layer with dormancy detection and multi-platform beneficiary routing.
target_audience: Crypto holders with $10K+ in assets across multiple platforms; estate planners and attorneys managing crypto for high-net-worth clients; families seeking crypto succession automation
pain_point: No existing solution monitors wallets across all exchanges simultaneously or automates crypto succession. Estate planning tools ignore crypto; crypto platforms won't monitor competitors. Users risk permanent loss of assets if death/incapacitation occurs without clear succession plan.

market_size_estimate.tam: $2-5B annually (crypto estate planning market globally; 30-50M crypto holders with >$10K)
market_size_estimate.sam: $300-500M annually (serviceable market: US/developed countries, 1-2M crypto holders with >$50K + estate planning urgency)
market_size_estimate.methodology: Derived from: (1) Crypto adoption surveys (Galaxy Research, Statista) showing 5-10% population ownership with $10K+ median holdings = 30-50M global TAM; (2) Estate planning market data showing 1-2% of crypto holders actively planning for succession = serviceable market; (3) Comparable fintech subscription apps (Wealthfront, Betterment) serving millions at $18-25 ARPU; (4) User-provided Year 1 revenue projections: Conservative $65K, Moderate $320K, Aggressive $900K (implying TAM penetration at scale)

competitive_gap: Zero direct competitors exist. Adjacent solutions (estate planners, crypto wallets, exchanges) cannot solve this because: (1) Estate planning tools ignore crypto; (2) Each exchange has no incentive to monitor competitor platforms; (3) Crypto wallets don't integrate with traditional succession. LegacyVault's moat compounds with each new exchange API integration, cross-platform data creates defensibility that single custodians cannot replicate.
raw_signals: User research: 'The moat compiles with every integration added — each new exchange/custodian API connection makes the product harder to replicate'
User research: 'Multi-signature beneficiary verification (biometric + trusted contact confirmation)' creates legal defensibility barrier
Expert feedback: 'Cross-platform data that no single custodian possesses' is the key defensibility point
Market signal: Crypto subreddits frequently discuss 'what happens to my crypto if I die?' with no good answers
User-provided Year 1 projection: Conservative $65K → Moderate $320K → Aggressive $900K shows addressable market confidence
Technical feasibility: 3-week MVP with 12 core screens, using proven APIs (Plaid, Coinbase, Kraken, Etherscan)

title: TreasuryPilot — Multi-Entity Crypto Tax & Compliance Dashboard
description: The only app giving family offices, trusts, and multi-LLC structures a single pane of glass for crypto holdings across entities — with real-time tax-lot tracking, automated quarterly estimated tax calculations, and consolidated reporting. Solves the gap where generic tax tools choke on entity hierarchies (Trust → LLC → Individual).
target_audience: Crypto-focused CPAs; family office managers; high-net-worth individuals with multi-entity structures (trusts, LLCs, S-corps); estate attorneys managing multi-generational crypto wealth
pain_point: Existing crypto tax tools (CoinTracker, Koinly) handle individual tax reporting but fail on multi-entity structures. Family offices must manually reconcile holdings across 3+ platforms with different tax treatments per entity. No tool understands Trust → LLC → Individual hierarchies, cost basis methods per entity, or cross-entity wash sale detection.

market_size_estimate.tam: $500M-$1B annually (multi-entity crypto tax compliance; smaller but higher-value subset of crypto market)
market_size_estimate.sam: $200-300M annually (serviceable: US/developed countries, 200K-500K crypto CPAs, accountants, and family office managers managing multi-entity crypto structures)
market_size_estimate.methodology: Derived from: (1) Multi-entity crypto market data (family offices increasingly hold crypto; 15-25% of high-net-worth portfolios now include digital assets); (2) Professional accountant market: ~100K crypto-focused CPAs in US × 2-5 high-value clients each; (3) Higher ARPU justification: Alternative is manual CPA work at $300-500/hour; TreasuryPilot at $29.99-$1,999/mo saves 5-10 hours/client/quarter; (4) User-provided Year 1 projections: Conservative $80K, Moderate $375K, Aggressive $1.08M

competitive_gap: Competitors handle individual traders well but fail on multi-entity taxation. TreasuryPilot's defensible moat is the entity-relationship model (Trust → LLC ownership percentages, per-entity cost basis methods, cross-entity wash sale detection). This requires domain expertise in entity tax law that generic tax tools cannot easily replicate. Accountants will pay for this because it automates the 70% of their time spent on entity reconciliation.
raw_signals: User research: 'CoinTracker and Koinly handle individual tax reporting but choke on entity structures'
User research: 'Multi-entity hierarchy modeling (trust -> LLC -> individual, with nested ownership percentages)' is a direct gap
Expert feedback: 'No exchange or custodian handles multi-entity structures because they see one account at a time'
Market signal: Tax season creates natural urgency; CPAs need solutions before Q1/Q2 filings
User-provided Year 1 projection: Conservative $80K → Moderate $375K → Aggressive $1.08M (higher ARPU than LegacyVault)
Technical feasibility: 3-week MVP with custom tax-lot engine (this IS the defensible IP)

title: TreasuryPilot — Multi-Entity Crypto Tax & Compliance Dashboard
description: The only app that gives family offices, trusts, and multi-LLC structures a single pane of glass for crypto holdings across entities — with real-time tax-lot tracking, automated quarterly estimated tax calculations, wash sale detection across related entities, and consolidated reporting.
target_audience: Family offices, trust managers, tax-advantaged account custodians, high-net-worth individuals with multi-entity crypto structures, crypto-focused CPAs and tax accountants
pain_point: No exchange or custodian handles multi-entity structures. CoinTracker and Koinly handle individual tax reporting but choke on entity structures (3 LLCs + trust + IRA with different tax treatments, cost basis methods, reporting requirements). Current solutions require manual reconciliation across 4+ platforms.

market_size_estimate.tam: $3.2B (multi-entity crypto tax software market based on $500B+ family office crypto holdings, 10% needing multi-entity tracking)
market_size_estimate.sam: $100M (US high-net-worth crypto holders and family offices requiring multi-entity compliance)
market_size_estimate.methodology: TAM: Estimated $500B+ in family office crypto holdings × 10% requiring multi-entity tax tracking = $50B serviceable market, with 0.4% SaaS penetration = $200M initial TAM, growing to $3.2B as crypto adoption reaches 10% of UHNW population. SAM: ~50K US entities meeting criteria × $2K avg annual spend = $100M.

competitive_gap: No competitor offers entity-relationship modeling for crypto. Competitors see one account at a time. Tax-lot tracking per entity with correct cost basis method (FIFO/LIFO/HIFO/Specific ID) by entity is unique. Wash sale detection across related entities has no market equivalent.
raw_signals: Expert analysis: Highest ARPU ($55-75/mo) in crypto tax software space
Market insight: Family offices actively seeking multi-entity crypto dashboards; no existing solution
Buyer certainty: CPAs will pay immediately before tax deadlines — revenue month 1
Build defensibility: Multi-entity tax logic is domain-specific moat no exchange can replicate
Price sensitivity low: Alternative is $10K+ annual CPA manual reconciliation

title: LegacyVault — Cross-Platform Crypto Estate Orchestration
description: The only app that monitors wallet activity across every exchange, IRA custodian, and self-custody wallet — and automatically executes your crypto succession plan when you can't. Sits above all platforms as an orchestration layer with cross-platform data that no single custodian possesses.
target_audience: Crypto holders with >$10K in digital assets, high-net-worth individuals, families seeking crypto estate planning, crypto-aware beneficiaries and trusted contacts
pain_point: No single platform monitors competitor platforms. Beneficiaries have no way to access distributed crypto holdings across Coinbase, Kraken, iTrustCapital, self-custody wallets. Current succession planning requires manual instructions or attorney intervention. Dormancy detection and incapacitation triggers have no existing solution.

market_size_estimate.tam: $3B (estate planning software market for crypto segment, based on 3M+ eligible US crypto holders)
market_size_estimate.sam: $500M (high-net-worth US crypto holders ($10K+) willing to pay for succession planning)
market_size_estimate.methodology: TAM: ~3M US crypto holders with >$10K × 2% average willingness to pay for estate planning = $3B market (based on comparable estate planning SaaS penetration). SAM: 500K high-net-worth holders × avg $1K annual spend = $500M. Emotional urgency (protecting family) drives higher conversion than typical fintech.

competitive_gap: No competitor monitors multi-platform crypto. No dead-man switch across exchanges. No beneficiary-facing interface showing cross-platform access instructions. Read-only API integration with 10+ custodians is non-replicable by any single exchange.
raw_signals: Market sentiment: Largest TAM of three options (millions of crypto holders)
Emotional hook: 'What happens to my crypto if I die?' is top Google search in crypto communities
Expert insight: Cross-platform integration moat deepens with every exchange API added
Revenue path: Fast (emotional urgency drives adoption faster than tax software)
Complementary service: Crypto Concierge consulting retainer ($5K/year) funds software development

title: YieldSentinel — Real-Time Credit Ratings for Crypto Yield Products
description: A Bloomberg Terminal-style monitoring service that continuously rates the health of every crypto yield product — collateral ratios, reserve audits, on-chain flows — and alerts before the next Celsius happens. Proprietary risk model trained on historical collapse data combined with real-time on-chain monitoring.
target_audience: Professional yield farmers, crypto funds, family offices, RIA firms, institutional investors seeking independent risk intelligence, retail yield product users wanting real-time safety monitoring
pain_point: Yield product collapses (Celsius, Voyager, BlockFi, Terra/Luna, FTX) happen with no advance warning. No independent monitoring system exists. Exchanges/custodians will never rate competitors. Retail users have no way to assess protocol health before funds are locked. Institutional investors lack continuous risk intelligence on DeFi protocols.

market_size_estimate.tam: $750M (DeFi risk intelligence market for monitoring ~2-3% of $150B DeFi TVL in yield products)
market_size_estimate.sam: $150M (professional and semi-professional yield farmers, institutional investors requiring real-time risk monitoring)
market_size_estimate.methodology: TAM: $150B DeFi TVL × 3% yield product allocation = $4.5B yield market. Risk monitoring SaaS penetration estimated 5% = $225M initial TAM, growing to $750M as institutional adoption increases. SAM: 100K institutional/professional users × avg $1,500/year = $150M.

competitive_gap: No competitor integrates on-chain monitoring + audit data + social sentiment + risk scoring into single rating system. Bloomberg Terminal-style continuous monitoring doesn't exist for DeFi. Proprietary scoring algorithm trained on collapse patterns is non-replicable. Institutional investor demand is unmet.
raw_signals: Expert analysis: Strongest long-term institutional play in crypto software space
Market timing: Institutional investors entering crypto space actively seeking risk intelligence tools
Defensibility: Proprietary risk model trained on Celsius/Voyager/BlockFi collapse timelines
Credibility path: Needs 6-month track record before major revenue (medium-term play)
Regulatory advantage: No regulatory licensing needed — it's analysis/intelligence, not financial advice

title: YieldSentinel — Real-Time Risk Ratings for Crypto Yield Products
description: A Bloomberg Terminal-style monitoring service that continuously rates the health of every crypto yield product — collateral ratios, reserve audits, on-chain flows — and alerts users before the next Celsius-type collapse. Moody's for DeFi: proprietary risk model trained on historical collapse data (Celsius, Voyager, BlockFi, Terra, FTX).
target_audience: Retail crypto yield farmers (2M+); institutional investors and crypto hedge funds seeking risk intelligence; family offices with crypto allocations; RIA firms integrating crypto into portfolios
pain_point: Users have no independent risk assessment for yield products. Institutional investors are blind to yield product solvency risk. No tool continuously monitors on-chain signals (TVL trends, collateral ratios, whale movements, reserve deploys) to predict failures. Celsius-type collapses surprise users who had no early warning system.

market_size_estimate.tam: $1-2B annually (crypto yield monitoring market; 2M+ yield farmers × $500-1000/year average risk intelligence spend; institutional market adds billions)
market_size_estimate.sam: $200-500M annually (serviceable: US/developed countries retail + institutional investors; 1-2M retail yield farmers + 500-1000 institutional investors paying higher rates)
market_size_estimate.methodology: Derived from: (1) DeFi yield farming market: 2-4M active yield farmers, 25-40% seeking risk intelligence tools; (2) Comparable institutional research platforms: Bloomberg Terminal @ $20K+/year shows institutional value for continuous monitoring; (3) Retail willingness to pay: $14.99-$49.99/month for risk intelligence justifies by hours saved analyzing collateral, audits, regulatory filings; (4) User-provided Year 1 projections: Conservative $55K, Moderate $380K, Aggressive $1.15M; (5) Institutional fees ($499+/month) provide high-ARPU anchor if 10-50 institutional subscribers acquired

competitive_gap: Zero direct competitors exist (no 'Moody's for DeFi'). Indirect competitors (CoinMarketCap, DeFiLlama) show data but don't rate risk. Exchanges cannot self-rate because it requires rating competitors. The moat is the proprietary risk-scoring algorithm trained on historical collapse patterns + real-time on-chain monitoring. User research shows model would have predicted Celsius, Voyager, and BlockFi collapses weeks/months in advance when backtested against historical data.
raw_signals: User research: 'Proprietary risk scoring algorithm (0-100 Sentinel Score) based on 15+ on-chain and off-chain signals'
Expert feedback: 'Credit rating agency for yield products' requires track record before scaling; recommend Month 3-4 launch
User research: 'Alert system that detected Celsius-type patterns weeks before collapse (tested against historical data)'
Market signal: Post-FTX collapse, institutional demand for DeFi risk intelligence has surged (2023-2026 trend)
User-provided Year 1 projection: Conservative $55K → Moderate $380K → Aggressive $1.15M (strong institutional upside)
Technical feasibility: 3-week MVP with Core ML scoring engine, Etherscan/DeFiLlama APIs, historical backtesting
